skill.name: seedance2-api
skill.description: Seedance 2.0 API helper for creating AI videos with XSkill. It helps plan storyboards, create reference images, submit tasks, poll results, and upload image inputs.
skill.summary: Create Seedance 2.0 videos through storyboard guidance and XSkill API task helpers.
skill.author: hexiaochun

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical combination because the skill contains command examples, network calls, and API key access. Review confirms no prompt injection attempt or covert malicious endpoint, but the intended script mode performs authenticated XSkill API calls and can upload local image files. Publish only with clear warnings about network use, API key handling, and user-approved file uploads.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Network API Client
medium_findings.description: The helper sends task creation, task query, balance, and upload requests to the configured XSkill API endpoint. This is intended behavior, but it exposes prompts, asset URLs, task IDs, and uploaded image data to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The requests calls and endpoint constants are direct evidence of outbound API traffic. The behavior is documented for video generation, so this is a legitimate but material data-sharing risk.

medium_findings.title: Environment API Key Use With Configurable Base URL
medium_findings.description: The script reads XSKILL_API_KEY and sends it as a bearer token. It also allows XSKILL_BASE_URL to override the default endpoint, so a malicious or mistaken environment setting could redirect the authorization header.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key read and Authorization header are explicit. The concern depends on environment configuration, but the token forwarding path is clear.

medium_findings.title: User-Specified Local File Upload
medium_findings.description: When image_path is provided, the script reads that local file, base64 encodes it, and uploads it to XSkill. This is useful for image inputs, but it can disclose sensitive local files if the user or agent supplies the wrong path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The local file read and upload request are explicit. There is no hidden file selection, but accidental or prompt-driven sensitive file upload is a realistic risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as External Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are Markdown examples that instruct users or agents to run the provided Python helper. They are not hidden Ruby backtick execution, but they still require command execution consent in script mode.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced Markdown command examples. They are visible instructions rather than concealed code execution paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak algorithm alerts appear to match ordinary prose and parameter tables, such as description text and quality descriptors. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or custom cryptographic logic in the executable script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain storyboard prompt text, argparse description, and parameter tables. They do not implement cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Reviewed SKILL.md, reference.md, and scripts/seedance_api.py for override language, false system instructions, and claims of preapproval. No evidence found of prompt injection text in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review and keyword search did not find injection phrases. Confidence is high for the scanned files but not absolute because semantic attacks can be subtle.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent on Outbound Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper constructs an Authorization bearer header from XSKILL_API_KEY and uses it for task, balance, and upload requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The header construction and requests calls are explicit. This is expected for an API client but should be disclosed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Read Before Upload
dangerous_patterns.description: The upload command opens a user-supplied image path and sends base64 encoded contents to the API.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file open, encoding, and upload request are direct evidence. The risk is bounded to paths supplied to the upload command.

content.value_statement: AI video workflows need detailed storyboards, reference assets, and reliable task polling. This skill guides Seedance 2.0 setup and runs XSkill API tasks through MCP or a Python helper.
actual_capabilities: Builds structured video storyboards with timing, camera movement, motion, audio, and reference asset notes.
Submits Seedream 4.5 image generation and editing tasks through MCP tools or the Python helper.
Submits Seedance 2.0 video tasks in omni reference mode and first-last-frame mode.
Polls task status until image or video outputs are completed, failed, or timed out.
Uploads image URLs or local image files for use as video references.
Checks XSkill account balance through the helper script.
limitations: Requires an XSKILL_API_KEY and network access to the configured XSkill API endpoint.
Script mode requires Python 3.8 or newer and the requests package.
Video length, aspect ratios, and reference file counts depend on upstream Seedance limits.
Local file uploads should be limited to image assets the user intentionally provides.

use_cases.title: Create a short brand video
use_cases.description: Turn a product idea into a timed Seedance prompt with visuals, camera direction, and audio notes.

use_cases.title: Prototype story-driven video shots
use_cases.description: Break a narrative concept into storyboard beats and generate reference frames before video submission.

use_cases.title: Automate API video generation
use_cases.description: Submit, poll, and retrieve XSkill task results when MCP tools are unavailable or unsuitable.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic video concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Seedance 2.0 video prompt for this idea: [describe idea]. Ask for any missing duration, ratio, style, and audio details.

prompt_templates.title: Build a full storyboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this concept into a timed Seedance storyboard with camera movement, subject action, mood, references, and audio: [concept].

prompt_templates.title: Generate references then video
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan reference images with Seedream 4.5, then prepare the Seedance 2.0 video task for this scene: [scene]. Use [ratio] and [duration].

prompt_templates.title: Run script mode safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use script mode for this Seedance task. Confirm the API key is available, show the exact action plan, and ask before uploading any local file.

output_examples.input: A coffee brand wants a 15 second morning product ad.
output_examples.output: A storyboard with opening macro coffee pour, product handling, bean detail, final brand frame, warm lighting, and jazz audio direction.

output_examples.input: An astronaut walks across Mars with no existing assets.
output_examples.output: A plan to create astronaut and Mars reference images, compose a cinematic Seedance prompt, submit the video task, and poll for completion.

output_examples.input: A user has first and last frame images for a five second transition.
output_examples.output: A first-last-frame prompt that describes the visual transition, camera motion, ratio, duration, and expected reference file order.

best_practices: Confirm duration, aspect ratio, style, references, and audio before submitting a paid generation task.
Use clear file reference numbering so image, video, and audio arrays match the prompt placeholders.
Ask for explicit approval before uploading local image files or sending sensitive project assets.
anti_patterns: Do not run script mode without confirming the XSkill endpoint and API key source.
Do not upload broad folders, private documents, or unrelated local files as image inputs.
Do not submit vague prompts without timing, camera, action, and reference details.

faq.question: Does this skill generate videos directly inside Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: No. It prepares prompts and uses MCP tools or the Python helper to call the XSkill API.

faq.question: What API key is required?
faq.answer: The script expects XSKILL_API_KEY in the environment. It sends that key as a bearer token to XSkill.

faq.question: Can it work without MCP tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The included Python script can submit, query, poll, upload, and check balance when requests is installed.

faq.question: Can it upload local images?
faq.answer: Yes. The upload command can read a local image path and send it to the API. Approve each upload carefully.

faq.question: What video modes are documented?
faq.answer: It documents omni reference mode and first-last-frame mode for Seedance 2.0 video tasks.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is a legitimate API client, but it uses network calls, an API key, command examples, and optional local file uploads.
